steps.title: Identify what the detail does
steps.content: The underlined phrase “heavy use of grass cover” follows the conjunction “as,” which signals a causal explanation: grass cover is the reason white-footed mice show no pattern of reducing activity under high lunar intensity. The function is therefore to explain an exception to a general pattern.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — It emphasizes an attribute of white-footed mice that enables them to respond to lunar intensity variations more rapidly than desert kangaroo rats and many other nocturnal mammals do.: Speed of response is never mentioned in the passage. This choice introduces a comparison (“more rapidly”) that does not appear in the text. Unsupported comparison error. ✗
  • Choice B — It identifies a characteristic of white-footed mice that explains why they do not reduce their activity under high lunar intensity like desert kangaroo rats and many other nocturnal mammals do.: Grass cover gives mice a different cost-benefit balance, which is precisely why they display no inhibition of activity. The detail explains the exception. ✓
  • Choice C — It presents a trait of white-footed mice that serves as evidence for the assertion that desert kangaroo rats are not the only nocturnal mammals whose activity is inhibited by high lunar intensity.: White-footed mice are NOT inhibited by lunar intensity — they are the exception. This choice gets the direction of the evidence backwards. ✗
  • Choice D — It describes a behavioral pattern displayed by white-footed mice that is inconsistent with how they would be expected to respond to high lunar intensity based on benefits and costs.: The passage says grass cover produces “a different balance of reward and risk” — still a benefits-and-costs framework. The mice’s behavior is consistent with benefits-and-costs logic, just with different inputs. ✗
That is the correct answer.
